page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARMv9-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The architecture allocates a single thread per core, providing dedicated compute resources. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. Memory capacity is 8.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance in data compression and decompression, with gzip compression reaching 30.32 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.43 GB/sec. General compute benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1866.0 and a multi-core score of 5938.0. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance bottleneck, as evidenced by a poor PassMark CPU encryption score of 750.19.

The c8gn.xlarge is designed for network-intensive, compute-focused workloads that benefit from ARM64 architecture. It is suitable for tasks such as static web serving, which achieved a throughput of 9.85 GB/sec, and database operations, processing 9.4 million Redis SET operations per second. Due to its low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak performance in large-scale LLM inference, it is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or complex AI workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a specialized hardware profile that balances dedicated ARM cores with high network throughput, making it a viable option for scale-out compute tasks without the overhead of local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded performance in data compression, with gzip compression reaching 30.32 GB/sec, alongside average multi-core and database processing capabilities, such as 9.4 million Redis SET operations per second. However, cryptographic operations and LLM inference represent performance limitations. This instance is suited for network-optimized compute tasks, static web serving, and lightweight database operations that leverage the ARMv9 architecture.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high baseline network bandwidth for efficient scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for network-heavy tasks
Strong single-threaded performance in gzip and bzip3 compression benchmarks
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and ARM64-native applications
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
